claims.claim: Quote: 'A multilingual agent should feel like the same character in every language. Inconsistent tone, pacing, or expressiveness breaks trust faster than minor recognition errors.'
claims.found: Lines 823-824: 'A multilingual agent should feel like the same character in every language. Inconsistent tone, pacing, or expressiveness breaks trust faster than minor recognition errors.' Quote text exact; cited line range (~829-831) lands on different content (escalation logic). Offset from two-column PDF parse.

claims.claim: Three persona-continuity practices: select voices with similar tonal characteristics; maintain consistent pacing and turn-taking; treat voice selection as a UX/brand decision
claims.found: Lines 827,829,830: 'Selecting voices with similar tonal characteristics across languages' / 'Maintaining consistent pacing and turn-taking behavior' / 'Treating voice selection as a UX and brand decision, not a technical one'. All three exact; cited ~832-837 lands on different bullets (Restricting inference, Gradually specializing).

claims.claim: Localization quote: 'Prompts, personas, and system messages should be authored directly in the target language. Relying on real-time translation introduces tone drift, syntactic artifacts, and cultural misalignment'
claims.found: Lines 845-847: 'Prompts, personas, and system messages should be authored directly in the target language. Relying on real-time translation introduces tone drift, syntactic artifacts, and cultural misalignment that compound over long interactions.' Exact (note trims trailing clause).

claims.claim: Effective localization requires language-native persona definitions, regionally appropriate politeness/formality norms, and localized acknowledgments + error handling
claims.found: Lines 850-852: 'Language-native persona definitions' / 'Regionally appropriate politeness and formality norms' / 'Localized acknowledgments, clarifications, and error handling'. Exact; cited ~846-848 lands on the prior quote's text.

claims.claim: Knowledge grounding must also be localized: retrieved content must be translated before synthesis when data language differs from user language
claims.found: Lines 853-855: 'Knowledge grounding must also be localized. If enterprise data exists in one language and the user speaks another, retrieved content should be translated and adapted before synthesis to avoid disruptive code-mixing.'

claims.claim: A 300 ms TTFA target in English must be the same in Japanese, where synthesis may be slower
claims.found: 300ms is the note's own illustrative example of 'consistent pacing'; ebook says only 'Maintaining consistent pacing and turn-taking behavior' with no numeric TTFA budget. Not misattributed (note does not credit the number to deepgram), but unverifiable as a source figure.

claims.claim: No OSS clone (Pipecat, LiveKit, Rapida) has a persona-consistency enforcement / cross-language consistency layer; ElevenLabs orchestration does not address multilingual persona
claims.cited: pipecat, agents (LiveKit), voice-ai (Rapida) clones + el-orchestration source
claims.found: grep 'persona' in pipecat/src + livekit-agents returns only unrelated hits (Tavus avatar persona, mem0, IVR); 'tonal'/'voice family'/'cross-language' only in TTS plugin docs. voice-ai (rapidaai) README confirmed; persona hits only in UI static pages. Negative architectural claim holds.

summary: All four deepgram-ebook quotes/lists are verbatim-exact in content, but every explicitly cited line range (~829-831, ~832-837, ~842-844, ~846-848) points to DIFFERENT content; the real text sits 6-20 lines away (823-824, 827-830, 845-847, 850-852). The offset is a two-column PDF-parse artifact, not a content error, so these are MISCITED (true-but-wrong-line), not WRONG. Corrected line numbers provided. The 300ms TTFA figure is the note's own illustrative example (UNVERIFIABLE, honestly framed, not misattributed). Negative prior-art claims spot-checked via grep across pipecat/livekit/rapida clones and hold. code_refs is empty. No WRONG claims -> PARTIAL.
